methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

data.narrative:
Nimble Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Simon Kalouche, is evaluated against a peer universe of 8 private companies and 1 public comparable in the same sector and stage. The target's last post-money valuation was $1.0B on $221M total capital raised to date.

Two valuation methods triangulate the current fair value. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C to the next funding stage, yielding $1.7B. Peer-median analysis across 8 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables—including Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), and Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B)—produces a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide range reflects dispersion in the robotics sector, particularly elevated multiples at early-stage generalist platforms.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median estimate of $1.7B. This implies modest upside from the $1.0B last round. Key uncertainties include the target's revenue scale and growth trajectory relative to peers, differentiation within the robotics subsector, and near-term funding environment for Series D capital. The peer set's heterogeneity—spanning industrial software to hardware-heavy automation—introduces material width in comparable multiples.
